title: [FIRRTL] Support special substitutions in assert intrinsics
body:
The following seems like it should work, but it doesn't:

``` firrtl
FIRRTL version 5.1.0
circuit Foo1 :
  public module Foo1 :
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : UInt<1>
    input a : UInt<1>

    node _T = eq(reset, UInt<1>(0h0))
    intrinsic(circt_chisel_ifelsefatal<format = "Assertion failed: In {{HierarchicalModuleName}}, a = %d\n", label = "chisel3_builtin">, clock, a, _T, a)
    node _T_1 = eq(reset, UInt<1>(0h0))
    when _T_1 :
      printf(clock, UInt<1>(0h1), "In {{HierarchicalModuleName}}, a = %d", a) : printf
```

This compiles to:

``` verilog
module Foo1(
  input clock,
        reset,
        a
);

  `ifndef SYNTHESIS
    always @(posedge clock) begin
      if (~reset & ~a) begin
        if (`ASSERT_VERBOSE_COND_)
          $error("Assertion failed: In {{HierarchicalModuleName}}, a = %d\n", a);
        if (`STOP_COND_)
          $fatal;
      end
      if ((`PRINTF_COND_) & ~reset)
        $fwrite(32'h80000002, "In %m, a = %d", a);
    end // always @(posedge)
  `endif // not def SYNTHESIS
endmodule
```

It seems like you should be able to use any special substitution in an assert intrinsic. However, this doesn't do the replacement.

This was created from the example here: https://github.com/chipsalliance/chisel/issues/5001#issuecomment-3146901413
